name: Seafood Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 10
1. Make pancake batter by mixing flour and salt in a bowl. Make a well in the center and pour in the egg, 4 tablespoons oil, and 1 cup milk. Whisk to smooth batter, then add remaining milk.
2. Remove any skin from monkfish, rinse, and cut into bite-size pieces. Rinse and pat dry scallops, cut in half. In a saucepan, pour 3/4 cup milk, add garlic and bring to simmer. Add all fish except shrimp. Simmer for 5-6 minutes, then add shrimp. When shrimp turn pink, remove all fish to a bowl and set aside. Strain fish into milk and reserve.
3. Melt butter in saucepan, add flour and stir for 2 minutes. Pour in reserved milk and cream. Continue to stir and simmer until smooth. Turn heat to low, add cheese, parsley, salt, and pepper. Add fish, mix well.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. Place a small frying pan or omelet pan over high heat. Lightly grease with oil. Beat pancake batter, then pour 3 tablespoons into the pan. Turn pan so bottom is covered evenly with batter and cook for 40-60 seconds. Turn and cook an additional 30-40 seconds.
6. Transfer pancakes to a large plate. Continue with remaining batter (enough filling for 10 pancakes). Place an equal amount of filling (about 3 tablespoons) in the center of each pancake, fold sides over filling, and arrange in an ovenproof dish large enough to hold pancakes.
7. Place in oven for 20 minutes, or until hot through. Serve immediately.
These thin pancakes conceal a tasty mixture of fish and will appeal to the mysterious side of the Cancerian. These can be served as a first course or luncheon dish with a green salad and chilled white wine.
